note: Strong-form maintenance test sits on top of the SQL surface lift. Score 164/189 (86.8%) -> 166/189 (87.8%): both `sqe:table-maintenance:v2` and `sqe:table-maintenance:v3` flip partial -> full. The matrix caveat had said `rewrite_data_files` was manifest-only, but the real Parquet re-encoding had shipped in MR !96 (8f6b682) and the row-count invariant + commit safety in MR !98. The new `rewrite_data_files_actually_compacts_parquet` test in `maintenance_procedures_test.rs` proves it end-to-end: ten separate INSERT statements create ten data files; `CALL system.rewrite_data_files(min_input_files => 5, target_file_size_bytes => 1073741824)` re-encodes them; assertions cover summary input_count > output_count, SELECT COUNT(*) preserved, and table_files TVF reports a smaller live file count after the rewrite. Same code path runs against V3 metadata (already covered by `v3_e2e::rewrite_data_files_on_v3_table`). Same finding pattern as the MoR DELETE audit two weeks earlier: the doc had been describing a state the code left behind, and the only person to notice was the person reading both at the same time. Five cells remain partial: `sqe:equality-deletes:v2` (Java Iceberg cross-engine verification still wanted alongside the existing Spark test), `sqe:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2/v3` (no live test against a Horizon endpoint), `sqe:hadoop-catalog:v2/v3` (read-only by design; writes are race-prone on object stores without atomic rename). Six cells remain `none`: all V3 advanced types blocked upstream (variant, shredded variant, geometry, vector, lineage, multi-arg transforms). SQL surface lift sits on top of the loader refactor and Phase O+ step 2. Score 163/189 (86.2%) -> 164/189 (86.8%): `sqe:jdbc-catalog:v3` flips partial -> full on the back of the new `jdbc_postgres_v3_table_format_version_roundtrip` live test in `crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs::sql_postgres`, which creates a `format-version=3` table through the JDBC backend, drops the in-memory handle, reloads, and asserts the metadata still reports V3. The same MR closed three other gaps that made the previous notes stale: `sqe:equality-deletes:v2` evidence now points at the Spark cross-engine read test (it is plain `#[test]` and self-skips when docker is absent, not `#[ignore]`); `sqe:table-maintenance:v2/v3` notes now list all four `CALL system.*` procedures with their dedicated live tests in `maintenance_procedures_test.rs` (only `rewrite_data_files` keeps a partial caveat for the Parquet-re-encoding gap). The MR also lifted the SQL surface for two Trino-compat features whose primitives already existed in Arrow + iceberg-rust: `JSON` columns now alias to `Utf8` so `CAST(json_col AS BIGINT|VARCHAR|DOUBLE)` rides DataFusion's built-in coercion, and `TIME` / `TIME(p)` columns now map to `Time64(Microsecond)` with `localtime()` returning Time64 and `hour/minute/second` EXTRACT bridges working on Time64 columns. Both changes are out of scope for the icebergmatrix.org rubric (they affect Trino-compat, not Iceberg-spec coverage), but they close two long-standing red items in `docs/trino-compatibility.md`. Loader refactor (feat/iceberg-loader-s3tables) sits on top of Phase O+ step 2. Score stays 163/189 (86.2%): no cells flipped levels, but every non-REST backend cell got cleaner evidence and the Glue / S3 Tables paths now have live AWS verification on the record. The shim crates `crates/sqe-catalog/src/backends/{glue,hms,sql}.rs` were deleted; their construction logic moved into the generic `iceberg-catalog-loader` crate (vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/loader/) which exposes a single `load(catalog_type, props)` factory used from `SessionCatalog::for_session_other_backend`. The S3 Tables native backend (vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/s3tables/, AWS SDK over the s3tables service) shipped in the same MR and is reachable through the same loader path with `[catalog.backend] type="s3tables"`. Live AWS validation: account 311141556126 in eu-central-1 returned `iceberg_demo_analytics.iceberg_user_events` (~1.5M rows) through the native Glue backend (`live_glue_namespace_round_trip`); the federated Glue Iceberg REST endpoint (eu-west-1) returned `testtablebucket/testnamespace/daily_sales` against an S3 Tables bucket (`s3_tables::list_namespaces_via_glue_rest`). The icebergmatrix.org rubric has no dedicated `s3-tables` cell, so S3 Tables verification rides in the `aws-glue-catalog` cells alongside the Glue native + REST coverage. Phase O+ step 2 sits on top of Phase R. Score 162/189 (85.7%) -> 163/189 (86.2%). The deferred backend-dispatch refactor closed the engine-wiring caveat that has been on the hms / glue / jdbc cells since Phase L. `SessionCatalog::for_session` now reads `[catalog].backend` from `SqeConfig` and dispatches to the matching iceberg::Catalog implementation: REST stays the default (legacy `polaris_url` keeps working), `Hms { uri, warehouse }` builds an `HmsCatalog`, `Glue { region, warehouse, endpoint }` builds a `GlueCatalog`, `Jdbc { url, warehouse }` builds a `SqlCatalog`. REST-specific methods on SessionCatalog (view DDL, raw `commit_schema_update`, ETag revalidation) error out with a clear message under non-REST backends. The `for_session_dispatches_through_jdbc_backend` integration test in crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs proves the engine-side dispatch end-to-end against live Postgres: the test builds an `SqeConfig` with `[catalog.backend] type=jdbc`, calls `SessionCatalog::for_session`, then exercises a namespace round-trip through the bridge that the coordinator hands to DataFusion. `sqe:jdbc-catalog:v2` flips partial -> full; `sqe:hive-metastore:v2/v3` and `sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v2/v3` keep their `full` rating with the engine-wiring caveat lifted. Phase R sits on top of Phase Q. Phase R flips bloom-filters v2 and v3 from partial to full by closing the last evidence gap: a self-contained footer-inspection unit test in parquet_writer_config (writer_props_emit_bloom_filter_in_parquet_footer) writes a parquet file via build_writer_props and re-reads it to confirm the bloom filter offset lands in the metadata. Combined with the existing 6 unit tests on property parsing and the v3 e2e test on catalog round-trip, the bloom write path is now verified end-to-end without needing the docker-compose stack. The earlier matrix note about a missing 'worker-side data writer' was misleading: SQE has no separate worker writer, all data-file writes go through the same parquet_writer_config helper. The bench-bloom-on-write experiment (feat/bench-bloom-on-join-keys at SF1/SF10) is documented in docs/features/runtime-filter-pushdown.md as a negative result; bloom-on-write does not compose with the runtime filter pushdown that landed in MR !112 because Path B-2 already prunes row groups that the bloom would address. Phase Q sits on top of Phase P. Phase Q flipped unity-catalog v2 and v3 from partial to full. Unity Catalog OSS exposes an Iceberg REST adapter at /api/2.1/unity-catalog/iceberg/ that the existing iceberg-catalog-rest client reaches with no AWS-style SigV4 (auth disabled in the OSS image; bearer-auth path against Databricks-hosted Unity stays separately covered by the OIDC M2M provider test). The OSS adapter is read-only at this version (list/load only; create/drop/commit not yet implemented per unitycatalog/unitycatalog#3), so the live test asserts the seeded `unity.default.marksheet_uniform` table comes back through list_namespaces and list_tables. docker-compose.unity.yml pins unitycatalog/unitycatalog:main-2f2e32d. Phase P sits on top of Phase O. Phase P added an `aws-sigv4` cargo feature to the vendored iceberg-catalog-rest crate that swaps the OAuth/Bearer authenticator for an AWS SigV4 signer when `rest.sigv4-enabled=true`; with it, the same code path that talks to Polaris and Nessie now reaches AWS S3 Tables through the federated Glue Iceberg REST endpoint. The capability lift is real: the vendored iceberg-catalog-rest client gained an `aws-sigv4` cargo feature (default-on for SQE) that swaps in an AWS SigV4 signer when `rest.sigv4-enabled=true` is set on user props or surfaces in the server's /v1/config defaults. With it, the same code path that talks to Polaris and Nessie now talks to AWS's federated Glue Iceberg REST endpoint (`glue.<region>.amazonaws.com/iceberg`, signing-name=glue) and the per-bucket S3 Tables endpoint (`s3tables.<region>.amazonaws.com/iceberg`, signing-name=s3tables). Verified live against a pre-existing S3 Tables bucket in eu-central-1: list_namespaces returned `table_demo_analytics`, list_tables returned `table_user_events`, all through SQE's Catalog trait with no AWS-specific call sites. Phase O background still applies: HMS v2/v3, Nessie v3, Glue v2/v3 all flipped to full earlier in the same branch via live tests. The session-manager wiring gap stays as a caveat on the SDK-based catalog paths (HMS, JDBC, native Glue); the federated REST endpoint reaches S3 Tables without that gap because it rides the existing REST code path.

sqe:position-deletes:v3.notes: MoR DELETE on V3 tables writes position-delete files alongside the existing data files; live data file count stays unchanged after DELETE (no CoW rewrite). Verified with crates/sqe-coordinator/tests/v3_e2e.rs::position_deletes_on_v3_table against docker-compose.test.yml.

sqe:equality-deletes:v2.notes: DELETE with write.delete.mode=merge-on-read writes equality-delete files via EqualityDeleteFileWriter and commits through RowDeltaAction. Unit tests assert file structure (3 data + 2 pos-delete + 1 eq-delete + Operation::Overwrite). Cross-engine read verification: `spark_reads_sqe_equality_delete_file` in equality_delete_integration.rs runs Spark 4.0.0 (Apache Iceberg's reference implementation, JVM-based) against an SQE-shaped MoR table and asserts the row count after delete. The test is plain `#[test]` and self-skips with a reason when docker-compose.spark.yml is not running, so it executes whenever the stack is up. The previous caveat about `RowDeltaOperation::delete_entries` returning `Ok(vec![])` was a misframed code-organization note: that return is the correct behaviour for RowDelta (see vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/iceberg/src/transaction/row_delta.rs:203-209 and the doc comment 'No custom manifest entries beyond what SnapshotProducer already generates via removed_data_file_paths filtering'). RowDelta only adds files (data + delete files); it does not remove existing data file entries (that is CoW's job through RewriteFilesAction). The empty Vec is the correct case, not a simplification. The `removed_data_file_paths` filtering happens through `SnapshotProducer::manifest_file` in transaction/snapshot.rs:503-580 with the optional `delete_filter_manager` (which `RewriteFilesAction` opts into via `set_enable_delete_filter_manager(true)` and `RowDeltaAction` does not need because it only adds, never removes). Spark IS Apache Iceberg's reference implementation; the existing Spark cross-engine read test verifies exactly what the previous caveat asked for ('independently verified against Java Iceberg').

sqe:equality-deletes:v3.notes: Equality-delete UPDATE on a V3 table with a declared identifier-field-id commits a single RowDelta with the new data file and the equality-delete row, then the SELECT returns the post-update value. Verified with crates/sqe-coordinator/tests/v3_e2e.rs::equality_delete_update_on_v3_table.
caveats: Cross-engine read test against Spark still ships as #[ignore].

sqe:merge-on-read:v2.notes: All three DML kinds (DELETE, UPDATE, MERGE) route through RowDeltaAction when write.*.mode = merge-on-read. UPDATE emits one data file per batch of matched rows plus an equality delete for old PKs. MERGE composes MATCHED UPDATE (data file + equality delete), MATCHED DELETE (equality delete only), and NOT MATCHED INSERT (data file only) into one RowDelta commit. Without a declared PK the dispatcher falls back to CoW for UPDATE and MERGE; DELETE falls back to position deletes.
caveats: Cross-engine read test (Spark 4.1, Trino 465) ships as #[ignore] pending docker-compose.spark.yml / docker-compose.trino.yml.
MoR benchmark harness at scripts/benchmark-mor-vs-cow.sh is scripted; live timings require the test stack.

sqe:merge-on-read:v3.notes: MoR DELETE writes position-delete files; MoR UPDATE writes data + equality-delete in one RowDelta when the V3 table declares an identifier-field-id. Both paths verified end-to-end against Polaris.
caveats: Cross-engine read test against Spark still ships as #[ignore].

sqe:copy-on-write:v2.notes: DELETE/UPDATE/MERGE via RisingWave iceberg-rust fork rewrite_files().

sqe:copy-on-write:v3.notes: DELETE on a V3 table without a declared MoR property runs CoW: rewrites the matched files via RewriteFilesAction, drops the row from subsequent SELECTs, and commits a fresh snapshot through Polaris.

sqe:schema-evolution:v3.notes: ADD COLUMN with DEFAULT lands the new field with both write_default and initial_default on a V3 table; the column appears in information_schema.columns after the commit. Verified end-to-end against Polaris.
caveats: iceberg-rust's TableScan reads the snapshot's declared schema rather than metadata.current_schema(), so retroactive initial_default fill on pre-evolution snapshots is not surfaced yet (read-side gap, not write-side).

sqe:type-promotion:v3.notes: ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... SET DATA TYPE widens columns on V3 tables (verified int -> bigint). The new type surfaces through information_schema.columns and the schema change commits through Polaris.
caveats: Nanosecond-to-microsecond narrowing not attempted (Iceberg spec rejects narrowing).

sqe:column-default-values:v3.notes: CREATE TABLE ... DEFAULT <literal> applies write_default; ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT applies initial_default. Function-call defaults rejected with clear error.

sqe:table-creation:v3.notes: CREATE TABLE auto-upgrades to format-version 3 when columns require V3 features (TIMESTAMP_NS, DEFAULT). Polaris materialises V3 metadata because SQE forwards format-version through the reserved table property (Iceberg REST has no dedicated format-version field on CreateTableRequest).

sqe:time-travel:v2.notes: FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF + 6 metadata TVFs (Step 8c).

sqe:time-travel:v3.notes: FOR VERSION AS OF works end-to-end on V3 tables: the classifier strips the clause, apply_version_spec registers a snapshot-pinned provider under datafusion.public.<alias>, and the SQL is rewritten to reference it. SELECT against the pinned snapshot returns the historical row count.

sqe:table-maintenance:v2.notes: All four CALL system.* procedures ship with both parser unit tests and live e2e tests that go through docker-compose.test.yml (Polaris + RustFS): `rewrite_data_files_returns_summary` plus `rewrite_data_files_actually_compacts_parquet`, `expire_snapshots_retain_last_commits`, `remove_orphan_files_respects_default_threshold`, `rewrite_manifests_commits_and_returns_summary`. The new compaction strong-form test inserts ten separate data files via ten INSERT statements, runs `CALL system.rewrite_data_files(min_input_files => 5, target_file_size_bytes => 1073741824)`, and asserts: input_count > output_count in the summary row, SELECT COUNT(*) preserved, table_files TVF shows fewer live files than before. rewrite_data_files does real Parquet re-encoding via `rewrite_group` -> `read_parquet_file` + `write_data_files`, with row-count invariant checked before commit and post-commit metadata-cache invalidation. Earlier matrix caveats describing it as manifest-only were stale; the real re-encoding shipped in MR !96 and the row-count invariant + commit safety in MR !98.

sqe:table-maintenance:v3.notes: CALL system.rewrite_data_files merges files on V3 tables with real Parquet re-encoding; row count preserved, file count drops, snapshot log moves forward. Same maintenance code path as V2: `rewrite_group` reads input data files via the table's FileIO, accumulates RecordBatches, and writes new Parquet files through the same `write_data_files` helper that powers INSERT and CTAS. Format-version 1/2/3 ride the same code path because re-encoding operates on Arrow batches, not on Iceberg metadata. expire_snapshots / remove_orphan_files / rewrite_manifests also work against V3 metadata. Verified by `rewrite_data_files_on_v3_table` (V3-specific) plus the strong-form `rewrite_data_files_actually_compacts_parquet` (10 small files -> fewer files, row count preserved, table_files TVF reports smaller live count).

sqe:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Transaction::{create_branch, create_tag, drop_branch, drop_tag} in vendored iceberg-rust + ALTER TABLE ... CREATE BRANCH/TAG DDL + SET WRITE_BRANCH session var. Main branch drop rejected.

sqe:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Same transaction actions apply to V3 tables; ref semantics are format-version agnostic.

sqe:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: CREATE TABLE ... PARTITIONED BY (...) accepts all six standard Iceberg transforms: identity (bare column), year/month/day/hour, bucket(N, col), truncate(L, col), and void(col). The clause is normalized from `PARTITIONED BY` to sqlparser's native `PARTITION BY` form by a pre-parser, then translated into an Iceberg UnboundPartitionSpec with explicit field IDs starting at 1000 (Polaris rejects null IDs). The writer uses TaskWriter + RecordBatchPartitionSplitter to compute partition values per row and route them to per-partition data files. Verified end-to-end against Polaris: identity, day, bucket, truncate, and 3-field composite specs all round-trip; INSERT + COUNT(*) + WHERE on the partition column return correct results.

sqe:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Same SQL syntax as V2 (PARTITIONED BY) and the same writer path. Verified on a V3 table with a TIMESTAMP_NS column partitioned by day(ts): INSERT routes per-row through the partition splitter and COUNT(*) returns the expected count.

sqe:partition-evolution:v2.notes: ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP/REPLACE PARTITION FIELD evolves the partition spec end-to-end. Pre-parser in sqe-sql/partition_evolution.rs lifts the non-standard syntax into a PartitionEvolution AST. The coordinator's apply_partition_evolution handler builds a new UnboundPartitionSpec from the current default spec, assigns continuing field ids from metadata.last_partition_id() + 1, and commits TableUpdate::AddSpec + SetDefaultSpec { spec_id: -1 } via the REST table-commit endpoint. Read path was already correct (per-file partition_spec_id from iceberg-rust). Writer was patched so the unpartitioned fast path stamps the current default spec_id when spec_id != 0.

sqe:partition-evolution:v3.notes: V3 metadata stores per-file partition_spec_id the same way V2 does; the same coordinator handler and writer path exercise V3 nanosec timestamp partitioning unchanged.

sqe:read-support:v3.notes: SELECT against V3 tables works through the existing iceberg-rust scan path: TIMESTAMP_NS columns materialise as Arrow Timestamp(Nanosecond), INSERT/SELECT round-trips, COUNT(*) and projections all return the expected results.
caveats: Variant/geometry not supported (separate cells track those gaps).

sqe:write-insert:v3.notes: INSERT into V3 tables with TIMESTAMP_NS columns commits a snapshot through Polaris and the rows materialise on subsequent SELECT. INSERT round-trip verified end-to-end.
caveats: Variant/geometry writes not supported (separate cells track those gaps).

sqe:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: All three DML ops via CoW rewrite_files().

sqe:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: DELETE (CoW + MoR position), UPDATE (MoR equality), and MERGE INTO (MATCHED UPDATE + NOT MATCHED INSERT) all commit on V3 tables. Each path has a direct V3 e2e test asserting the post-state through the catalog.

sqe:catalog-integration:v3.notes: V3 tables created by SQE materialise correctly in the Iceberg REST catalog: CREATE/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE all commit, table metadata reloads carry the right format-version, and Polaris accepts V3-only column types because format-version is forwarded as a table property.

sqe:statistics:v2.notes: Column metrics used for pruning and CBO.

sqe:statistics:v3.notes: SHOW STATS FOR works on V3 tables, returning per-column metrics from the active snapshot. Same DataFusion + iceberg-rust scan path as V2; V3 metadata participates without modification.

sqe:bloom-filters:v2.notes: All SQE data-file write paths (coordinator CTAS, streaming INSERT, MERGE, rewrite_data_files) build WriterProperties through the shared parquet_writer_config helper, which honours write.parquet.bloom-filter-columns and write.parquet.bloom-filter-fpp. There is no separate worker-side data writer today; every distributed write that lands also goes through the same helper. The full chain is now under test: 6 unit tests prove property parsing -> WriterProperties translation, the v3 e2e test proves the property round-trips through Polaris (CREATE TABLE persists, SHOW CREATE TABLE re-emits), and a self-contained footer-inspection test (writer_props_emit_bloom_filter_in_parquet_footer) writes a parquet file via build_writer_props and re-reads it to confirm the bloom filter offset lands in the metadata. DataFusion reads blooms automatically at scan time. Puffin theta-sketch sidecar emitted via puffin_stats. CALL system.suggest_bloom_filter_columns analyses query history.
caveats: DataFusion StatisticsSource consumer for Puffin NDV is blocked on DF 54 (apache/datafusion#21157); this affects the cardinality-estimation path that feeds blooms, not the bloom write/read itself.

sqe:bloom-filters:v3.notes: V3 tables accept the bloom-filter property at CREATE TIME, the property survives the round-trip through Polaris, and SHOW CREATE TABLE re-emits it. Writer path is format-version agnostic and uses the same parquet_writer_config helper as V2. The footer-inspection test in parquet_writer_config (writer_props_emit_bloom_filter_in_parquet_footer) is shared between V2 and V3 because the parquet writer behaviour is identical regardless of Iceberg format version; the v3 e2e test exercises the catalog round-trip path against a V3-format table.
caveats: Same DF 54 NDV consumer block as V2 (apache/datafusion#21157); does not affect bloom write/read.

sqe:hive-metastore:v2.notes: iceberg-catalog-hms vendored from apache/iceberg-rust v0.9.0 into vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/hms/, wired behind the `hms` cargo feature (now in default). Phase O brought up an apache/hive:standalone-metastore-4.1.0 container via docker-compose.hms.yml and proved the round-trip end-to-end: hms_namespace_round_trip in crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs creates, lists, and drops a namespace through the live Thrift surface using HmsBackend::build_catalog. Phase O+ step 2 closed the engine-wiring gap: `SessionCatalog::for_session` now dispatches on `[catalog].backend`, so a coordinator configured with `[catalog.backend] type=hms` routes SQL through the HMS catalog instead of the REST client.

sqe:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Same vendored iceberg-catalog-hms code path as V2 plus the format-version property forwarding proven on Polaris (HMS itself is format-version agnostic). Live Thrift round-trip verified against apache/hive:standalone-metastore-4.1.0 via docker-compose.hms.yml. Phase O+ step 2 wired engine-level dispatch (see V2 cell).

sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v2.notes: Two parallel paths now ship: (1) iceberg-catalog-glue vendored from apache/iceberg-rust v0.9.0 (AWS SDK over the Glue API directly); (2) the federated Glue Iceberg REST endpoint at https://glue.<region>.amazonaws.com/iceberg, reachable through the vendored iceberg-catalog-rest client with Phase P's SigV4 auth. Path (1) verified by live_glue_namespace_round_trip in eu-central-1: create_namespace -> list_namespaces -> drop_namespace through GlueBackend::build_catalog using the standard AWS provider chain. Path (2) verified by list_namespaces_via_glue_rest in the same test file, listing namespaces and tables in a pre-existing S3 Tables bucket through SigV4-signed REST. Phase O+ step 2 closed the engine-wiring gap on path (1): `SessionCatalog::for_session` dispatches on `[catalog].backend` so a coordinator configured with `[catalog.backend] type=glue` routes SQL through the GlueCatalog. Credentials and warehouse identifiers flow through .env (template at .env.example) so no profile names land in source.
sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v2.evidence: vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/glue/, vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/s3tables/, vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/loader/ (iceberg_catalog_loader::load("glue"|"s3tables")), crates/sqe-catalog/src/rest_catalog.rs#for_session_other_backend, crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs::glue::live_glue_namespace_round_trip (live AWS account 311141556126, eu-central-1, iceberg_demo_analytics.iceberg_user_events ~1.5M rows), crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs::s3_tables::list_namespaces_via_glue_rest (live S3 Tables eu-west-1, testtablebucket/testnamespace/daily_sales), vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/rest/src/sigv4.rs

sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: Same two parallel paths as V2: native AWS SDK via iceberg-catalog-glue, and the federated Glue Iceberg REST endpoint with SigV4 signing. Both are format-version agnostic at the catalog layer; format-version: 3 is forwarded through the Iceberg metadata.json which the catalogs treat as opaque. Live AWS round-trip verified in Phase O (SDK path) and Phase P (REST path against an existing S3 Tables bucket). Phase O+ step 2 wired engine-level dispatch (see V2 cell).
sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v3.evidence: vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/glue/, vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/s3tables/, vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/loader/ (iceberg_catalog_loader::load("glue"|"s3tables")), crates/sqe-catalog/src/rest_catalog.rs#for_session_other_backend, crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs::glue::live_glue_namespace_round_trip (live AWS account 311141556126, eu-central-1), crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs::s3_tables::list_namespaces_via_glue_rest (live S3 Tables eu-west-1, testtablebucket/testnamespace/daily_sales)

sqe:rest-catalog:v2.notes: Primary catalog type. Phase P added optional AWS SigV4 signing to the vendored `iceberg-catalog-rest` client (cargo feature `aws-sigv4`, on by default in SQE), letting the same code path talk to AWS's Iceberg REST endpoints (`glue.<region>.amazonaws.com/iceberg` and the per-bucket `s3tables.<region>.amazonaws.com/iceberg`). Authenticator reads the standard AWS credential chain, swaps in for the OAuth/Bearer flow when `rest.sigv4-enabled=true` lands in user props or the server's /v1/config defaults.

sqe:rest-catalog:v3.notes: Iceberg REST catalogs accept V3 tables when SQE forwards format-version as a table property in CreateTableRequest. Verified end-to-end against Polaris (Apache Polaris is REST-spec compliant). Phase P SigV4 patch makes the same V3 forwarding work against AWS S3 Tables through the federated Glue Iceberg REST endpoint.

sqe:nessie:v2.notes: Works via iceberg-catalog-rest against Nessie's REST adapter; documented in docs/deployment.md Section 5b.
caveats: Write parity depends on Nessie's REST adapter completeness.

sqe:nessie:v3.notes: Nessie speaks Iceberg REST. Phase O brought up ghcr.io/projectnessie/nessie:0.107.5 via docker-compose.nessie.yml (the 0.76.x image line shipped a partial REST adapter that 404'd on /iceberg/v1/config; 0.107.5 is the first tag where the Iceberg REST surface is fully usable) and verified the round-trip: nessie_namespace_round_trip in crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs builds the iceberg-catalog-rest client against http://localhost:19121/iceberg/, then create_namespace -> list_namespaces -> drop_namespace through Nessie's REST adapter. Same client SQE uses against Polaris in production.

sqe:polaris:v3.notes: Apache Polaris 1.3.0-incubating round-trips V3 tables when SQE sets format-version in the CreateTableRequest properties map. CREATE/INSERT/SELECT/DELETE/UPDATE all verified against the test stack.

sqe:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Unity Catalog OSS exposes an Iceberg REST adapter at /api/2.1/unity-catalog/iceberg/ that SQE reaches through the same iceberg-catalog-rest client used for Polaris. Read-only on the OSS side (list_namespaces / list_tables / load_table; create/drop/commit are not implemented per unitycatalog/unitycatalog#3), so the live test is a read-only smoke against the bundled `unity.default.marksheet_uniform` table that ships with the unitycatalog/unitycatalog:main-2f2e32d image. The OIDC M2M auth provider for Databricks-hosted Unity (where bearer auth is enforced) lives in sqe-auth and is exercised independently by the unity_catalog_m2m_auth_obtains_token test.
caveats: Write-path verification on Unity OSS waits on unitycatalog/unitycatalog#3 (create/drop/commit endpoints); the iceberg-rust client side is already proven against Polaris.

sqe:unity-catalog:v3.notes: Same vendored iceberg-catalog-rest code path as V2 plus the format-version: 3 forwarding pattern proven on Polaris. Unity Catalog OSS is format-version agnostic at the REST layer; the table metadata it serves is whatever the writer produced. Read-only round-trip verified live against the bundled `unity.default.marksheet_uniform` table.

sqe:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: Horizon is Polaris-based; REST wiring compatible; no live test.

sqe:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: Snowflake Horizon is Polaris-based; the REST surface is shared with Polaris which we verified end-to-end at format-version 3. No live test against a Horizon endpoint.
caveats: No live integration test against Horizon.

sqe:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: Storage-only scanner walks warehouse paths for metadata/v*.metadata.json (feature `hadoop`). Read path only; writes defer to REST/HMS.
caveats: Write path is race-prone on object stores without atomic rename; intentionally read-oriented.

sqe:hadoop-catalog:v3.notes: The hadoop-catalog scanner walks `metadata/v*.metadata.json`; format-version 1/2/3 are read by the same iceberg-rust deserializer. Read parity with V2 holds. Write path remains intentionally read-oriented (race-prone on object stores).
caveats: Read-only on V3 too; writes still defer to REST/HMS.

sqe:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: iceberg-catalog-sql vendored from apache/iceberg-rust v0.9.0 (vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/sql/) uses sqlx::any to dispatch to SQLite/PostgreSQL/MySQL. The legacy rusqlite helper in `backends/sql.rs` was removed in the loader-refactor MR; the generic `iceberg-catalog-loader` crate now constructs the right `iceberg::Catalog` impl from a `(catalog_type, props)` pair, which keeps `SessionCatalog::for_session` agnostic to the backend. Phase O+ step 2 had already closed the engine-wiring gap: `SessionCatalog::for_session` dispatches on `[catalog].backend`, building a JDBC `SqlCatalog` (or HMS / Glue / S3 Tables) when configured rather than always REST. The `for_session_dispatches_through_jdbc_backend` test in crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs builds an `SqeConfig` with `[catalog.backend] type=jdbc`, calls `SessionCatalog::for_session`, then exercises a namespace round-trip through the bridge that the coordinator hands to DataFusion. The standalone `jdbc_postgres_namespace_roundtrip` test continues to cover the library layer.
caveats: Live test exercises namespace ops; create_table through SessionCatalog needs a writable warehouse FileIO that the test environment does not stand up; covered by the upstream iceberg-catalog-sql tests.

sqe:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: iceberg-catalog-sql vendored alongside the fork is format-version agnostic; the format-version: 3 property forwarding pattern proven on Polaris works identically here. SQLite, PostgreSQL, and MySQL all accept V3 metadata. Phase O+ step 2 added engine-level dispatch through JDBC; the loader-refactor MR added a V3 round-trip live test (`jdbc_postgres_v3_table_format_version_roundtrip`) that creates a table with `format-version=3` through the JDBC backend, drops the in-memory handle, reloads, and asserts the metadata still reports V3. Closes the engine-wiring caveat that has been on the cell since Phase L.

sqe:variant-type:v3.notes: Deferred to next cycle. iceberg-rust #2188 not merged.

sqe:vector-type:v3.notes: Deferred. Iceberg V3 vector spec not finalised.

sqe:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: TIMESTAMP_NS(N) and TIMESTAMPTZ_NS(N) DDL route to PrimitiveType::TimestampNs/TimestamptzNs; Arrow bridge and INSERT/SELECT wiring covered by v3_types_integration.

sqe:cdc-support:v3.notes: FOR INCREMENTAL BETWEEN SNAPSHOT works end-to-end on V3 tables: pre-classification strips the clause, the planner registers an IncrementalTableProvider, and the scan returns the rows added in the requested range. Verified against Polaris.
caveats: Snapshot id ordering: the validator currently uses numeric comparison (start > end) rather than parent-chain ancestry. Random Iceberg snapshot ids can fall in either order; the start = 0 sentinel sidesteps the issue. Tracked separately.
